LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : PLAINTIFF JANET PICKENS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. IT DOES NOT CONTAIN A MEMORANDUM OF POINT AND AUTHORITIES OR ANY SUPPORTING EVIDENCE AS REQUIRED BY C R C 3.1350(C) AND THE SEPERATE STATEMENT DOES NOT INCLUDE EVIDENTIARY REFERENCES AS REQUIRED BY C R C 3.1350 (H). IN ANY EVENT IT IS PREMISED ON A DEFAULT JUDGMENT THAT HAS BEEN SET ASIDE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS : DEFENDANT JOHN STEWART COMPANY'S MOTION FOR ORDER REQUIRING PLAINTIFF TO FURNISH SECURITY C C P 391.1 IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF MUST POST SECURITY IN THE AMOUNT OF $500.00, IF SHE WISHES TO PURSUE THIS ACTION. PLAINTIFF IS A VEXATIOUS LITIGANT WHO HAS REPEATEDLY FILED UNMERITORIOUS MOTIONS IN THIS ACTION AND WHO HAS NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)

MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Default Prove-Up Hearing this day.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Patrick Heard, #11055.
Plaintiff, Janet Pickens, was present in pro per, placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by Defendant John Stewart Company.

After hearing the evidence presented, the Court takes the matter under submission.

Default Prove-Up.
Off Calendar Nov-21-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Commissioner Sue M. Kaplan, Clerk: Sajja Ravinantapricha & Carla Amador, CSR: Patricia Abalos #13128 (Department 218)

Appearances
Plaintiff, Janet Pickens, is present in propia persona.

Defendants, John Stewart Company, is not present.
(Default was entered on 11/8/07 as to John Stewart Company).

This cause came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing. Janet Pickens is sworn and examined by the Court.

After hearing the testimony and considering the evidence presented by plaintiff, the Court takes the matter under submission. Ms. Pickens submits a proposed Judgment to the Court.

**************************
On November 21, 2007, the Court orders a Default Judgment as follows:

Judgment is for plaintiff, Janet Pickens, and against defendant, John Stewart Company. Defendant must pay plaintiff $0.0, as and for damages, for a total of $0.0.
